description:
Advances: mutagen.coverage, mutagen.mutation_pipeline

Closes **F23** (HIGH, Performance F-PERF-20). Baseline (lib/mutagen_ex/baseline.ex:114-127) runs the full test suite once; coverage_runner.ex:236-287 then runs it again under `:cover`. Two full suite runs pre-mutation. `:cover` overhead is 10-50% on top. Combine into one instrumented run.

**Dependencies**: This ticket likely depends on .25 (extended AST cache + facade clean-up makes the merge tractable) and possibly on the outcome of .19 (if Option A — fold ecto into here).

## Deliverable
- Combine baseline + coverage into one `:cover`-instrumented test run. Capture coverage data AND baseline failures from the same execution.
- If baseline fails (any test fails not because of mutation), surface as `:baseline_red` per existing r? contract. Coverage data still useful for diagnosis even when baseline is red — preserve it.
- Update relevant specs: coverage.spec.md, mutation_pipeline.spec.md.

## Allowed touches
- lib/mutagen_ex/baseline.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex (orchestration sites)
- .spec/specs/coverage.spec.md, mutation_pipeline.spec.md
- Corresponding test files
- README.md, CHANGELOG.md

## Out of Scope
- Replace `:cover` with `:ex_cover` or any alternate library — `:cover` is the project standard
- Skip baseline entirely under coverage — baseline-red protection (r? in mutation_pipeline spec) must remain

## Merge gate
- [ ] mix test green
- [ ] Pre-mutation wall-clock halves (or measurably less) on representative scope
- [ ] Baseline-red protection still triggers
- [ ] Coverage map still byte-identical for legitimate cases (determinism)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE
title: Combine baseline + coverage into one instrumented run
